name: gmail-get-message
description:
Fetch ONE Gmail message (or a small labeled set) with the FULL detail an email-reply channel needs that Gmail: List Messages does NOT return: the plain-text body, the routing headers (To / Message-ID / In-Reply-To), the thread_id, and a parsed Authentication-Results verdict (SPF / DKIM / DMARC) used to gate sender trust.

Two modes: set MESSAGE_ID to fetch one specific message, OR set QUERY (e.g. 'label:Ask is:unread') + MAX_RESULTS to list then fetch each. Fetches format=full and extracts the text/plain MIME part (falls back to the snippet), capped at MAX_BODY_BYTES on a UTF-8 char boundary.

This is the 'read the email my assistant was sent' leg of the email-your-assistant channel. Chains cleanly into Gmail: Modify (mark read / label) via the shared messages[].id output shape.

Requires a vault:// reference to a Google OAuth access token in the AUTH_HEADER config key. The controller resolves + refreshes the token at dispatch time.

AUTH_HEADER.description: vault:// path to the OAuth access token wrapped in 'Bearer ' (e.g. 'Bearer vault://oauth/gmail/{user_id}/{email}/access_token'). The controller resolves the vault reference and refreshes the token if expired.

MESSAGE_ID.description: Mode A: fetch this one Gmail message id. If set, QUERY / MAX_RESULTS are ignored. Provide MESSAGE_ID or QUERY — at least one is required.

QUERY.description: Mode B: Gmail search query (same syntax as the web UI, e.g. 'label:Ask is:unread', 'from:me@x.com newer_than:1h'). Lists matching ids then fetches each in full. Ignored when MESSAGE_ID is set.

MAX_RESULTS.description: QUERY mode only: maximum messages to fetch in full (1-10). Each match is one extra full-body HTTP call — keep tight for fuel.

MAX_BODY_BYTES.description: Cap on extracted body_text bytes. Truncated on a UTF-8 char boundary so multi-byte codepoints are never split.

TRUSTED_AUTHSERV.description: The receiving authority whose Authentication-Results header we trust (the authserv-id, e.g. Gmail stamps 'mx.google.com'). Only verdicts from a header whose authserv-id equals this value OR ends with '.<value>' (dot-boundary suffix) feed spf_pass/dkim_pass/dmarc_pass. This defeats sender spoofing: an attacker's own injected Authentication-Results header carries a foreign authserv-id and is ignored. Change only for a self-hosted MTA.

setup_instructions.title: Enable the Gmail API
setup_instructions.detail: In https://console.cloud.google.com, enable the Gmail API and configure OAuth consent with the 'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly' scope (format=full needs read access to bodies — gmail.metadata is NOT sufficient for this module).

setup_instructions.title: Authorize the integration
setup_instructions.detail: Complete the OAuth flow via your Talos integration UI or CLI. The controller stores the access_token + refresh_token at 'oauth/gmail/{user_id}/{email}/access_token' and 'oauth/gmail/{user_id}/{email}/refresh_token', and tracks expiry in integration_credentials.

setup_instructions.title: Configure AUTH_HEADER
setup_instructions.detail: Set AUTH_HEADER to 'Bearer vault://oauth/gmail/{your_user_id}/{your_email}/access_token'. The controller resolves the vault path + refreshes the token before dispatch. If you hit 401s, call refresh_oauth_token(vault_path) to force-refresh and read the outcome.

setup_instructions.title: Gate sender trust on the auth verdict
setup_instructions.detail: Each message carries an 'auth' object: { spf_pass, dkim_pass, dmarc_pass, trusted, raw_present }. The verdicts are computed ONLY from an Authentication-Results header stamped by TRUSTED_AUTHSERV (default google.com) — a header an attacker injected into the message they sent carries a foreign authserv-id and is ignored, so 'trusted' is false and all three verdicts are false. In the email-your-assistant channel, refuse to act unless BOTH trusted==true AND dmarc_pass==true, so a spoofed 'from your own address' email can't drive the assistant.
